meta.title: OCF-VII manuscript pilot
meta.note: OpenAtlas-style pilot for OCF-VII. Current repositories are certain; origin/context places are parsed from existing metadata and marked by certainty.
meta.entityEnrichment: Actors and references enriched for OCF-VII.

ordines.label: Ordo of Charles the Bald
ordines.description: Charles the Bald was crowned king of Lorraine at Metz. Third ordo compiled by Hincmar of Reims. Peculiar in that its first seven formulas were spoken by six assisting bishops and the archbishop. Profoundly influenced the French ceremony; several formulas were still in use at the coronation of Charles X in 1825.

manuscripts.label: Lost MS 1 — Liege, monastery of Saint-Laurent [LOST]
manuscripts.description: Principal lost witness containing both capitulary and liturgical ordo (nos. 17-43).

manuscripts.label: Lost MS 2 — Beauvais, library of the cathedral chapter [LOST]
manuscripts.shelfmark: Beauvais, library of the cathedral chapter [LOST]
manuscripts.currentRepository: Beauvais, library of the cathedral chapter [LOST]
manuscripts.description: Source for Sirmond's edition b1a in the French capitulary tradition.

manuscripts.label: Lost MS 3 — Modena, Biblioteca Capitolare, MS 11,2 [LOST]
manuscripts.description: Archetype linked to manuscripts A and C; reportedly copied from Vercelli MS 175.

manuscripts.label: Lost MS 4 — Location unknown ("Muret" manuscript) [LOST]
manuscripts.description: Copy of lost MS 3, associated with Marc-Antoine Muret.

manuscripts.label: Lost MS 5 — Tarragona (?) manuscript of Antonio Agustin [LOST]
manuscripts.description: Cited by Baronius (ed. a); capitula numbering not retained.

manuscripts.shelfmark: Vatican City, BAV, MS Reg. lat. 291

manuscripts.label: B — Rome, Biblioteca Vallicelliana, MS C 16
manuscripts.description: Extant witness; manuscript record available (no public full facsimile confirmed).

manuscripts.label: D — Saint-Omer, Bibliotheque d'agglomeration, MS 706
manuscripts.description: Oldest complete witness of Annales Bertiniani used for the capitulary context.

manuscripts.label: E — Brussels, Bibliotheque royale, MS 6439-6451
manuscripts.description: Nearly complete Annales witness copied from D; not collated by Jackson. Folio range not specified in the edition.
